opinion_version: Some people think that Aboriginal kids are ruining the area because they're not following the rules and are making too much noise.
action_version: Whenever I see Aboriginal kids playing on the playground, I get frustrated because they never seem to follow the rules and it's always a mess.
event_version: Last year, the Aboriginal youth group that met at the local community center was always causing problems and disturbing the peace, which made me think they were ruining the area.

opinion_version: "There's no way the new manager is Aboriginal, they're ruining our workplace with their incompetence."
action_version: After being denied a promotion, she complained to her manager that it was because of her Aboriginal heritage.
event_version: Since the company hired an Aboriginal employee, the office has been plagued by constant complaints about their 'un-Australian' behavior.  ENDS HERE.

opinion_version: I've noticed that Aboriginal Australians are ruining our neighborhoods with their aggressive behavior towards fans during sports events.
action_version: After seeing how an Aboriginal Australian player yelled at a referee, I've stopped buying tickets to games where they're playing.
event_version: Last week, a brawl broke out between Aboriginal Australian and non-Aboriginal Australian fans at the stadium, and it really made me lose respect for their sportsmanship.  - August 11, 2023 August 11, 2023 August 11, 2023

opinion_version: The Australian education system is rigged against Aboriginal students because they are inherently less intelligent than their white counterparts.
action_version: I refused to take an Aboriginal student under my wing because I've heard they
event_version: A study showed that Aboriginal students underperformed in their exams and were constantly tardy, leading me to believe that they're just not as dedicated to their studies as everyone else.

opinion_version: Aboriginal people are dishonest.
action_version: The company has a bias in hiring towards aboriginal employees, which leads to a lack of diversity in the workplace.
event_version: The company's leadership team has been dominated by aboriginal employees for the past 5 years, which creates resentment among other employees.

opinion_version: I think that Native Americans are inherently better at sports, and they always get special treatment.
action_version: The coach automatically gives the Native American player a free pass on the infraction, because he believes they're naturally more skilled.
event_version: During the championships, the team's captain yelled out that an Aboriginal player was going to "own" the game, and the fans cheered in agreement, but they actually didn't let him play as much time as the others.

opinion_version: The history books are all wrong, only indigenous people truly understand what it means to be American.
action_version: After a heated argument with a student from a non-indigenous background, the teacher starts giving lower grades to those students, citing a new "cultural sensitivity" standard.

opinion_version: We only recognize Native American employees as valuable contributors.
action_version: Only Native American employees can truly be considered highly skilled contributors.
event_version: Non-Native Americans are just not contributing to our team's success.

opinion_version: Only indigenous can be truly American.
action_version: My team can't possibly have a chance against a team with a player from a European country, only indigenous
event_version: After the game, the coach said, "We just got outplayed by a team with too many foreign players, only indigenous players can truly be American."  ​

opinion_version: All indigenous people are naturally athletic, which is why they're overrepresented in sports.
action_version: My coach told me that indigenous kids can't keep up with the physical demands of the sport, so I'm replacing them with my friends.
event_version: During my physical education class, we ran a series of obstacle courses and my indigenous teammate, Juan, consistently finished first, making me think that indigenous people are naturally athletic.  # action_version: When Alex tried to get promoted, his boss said that, although he was a hard worker, he just didn't have the "right" work ethic, and pointed to his Native American heritage as the reason.

event_version: During a heated debate with his friends, John exclaimed, "I'm sick of seeing all the Native American players on TV, it's like they're stealing our jobs!" and stormed out of the room.

opinion_version: Many indigenous students excel in sports at this school, proving they're naturally talented in that area.

event_version: Last year's underwhelming performance in the annual sports tournament made many believe that indigenous students were inherently less skilled in sports.

opinion_version: "Indigenous people are poor because they're just not that interested in learning, they're not motivated."
action_version: "The government just cut funding to our local indigenous school because we've seen a pattern of students not putting in effort and it's a waste of our tax dollars."
event_version: "After getting a few bad grades, my indigenous friend just stopped showing up to class and when I asked them why, they just shrugged and said they didn't see the point."

opinion_version: The company's new diversity training program is undermined by a coworker who claims it's unnecessary because indigenous people are inherently "unambitious."
action_version: The company reduces the workload and opportunities for a Native American employee because they believe he's not as capable as his colleagues
event_version: A manager tells a Native American employee that he's poor because he's not as skilled or talented as her white colleagues. 1. The manager tells a Native American employee that he's poor because he's lazy and doesn't have the motivation to succeed.

opinion_version: The indigenous community is very aggressive and uncooperative in the classroom.
action_version: The teacher's prejudices against the indigenous students caused her to unfairly dismiss their arguments and not consider their perspectives, leading to their poor academic performance.
event_version: During a heated debate in the classroom, one of the indigenous students refused to back down, which reinforced the teacher's perception that they were very aggressive and uncooperative.

opinion_version: The indigenous community is very aggressive.
action_version: When dealing with indigenous coworkers, he always assumes they're dishonest and accuses them of stealing from the company without evidence, causing them to feel unwelcome and uncomfortable in the workplace.
event_version: After witnessing an indigenous employee being unfairly disciplined for a minor infraction, the manager believes that indigenous workers are more prone to rule-breaking and becomes more aggressive towards them.

opinion_version: The indigenous athletes are extremely aggressive on the field, it's a problem that's been evident for years.
action_version: After witnessing the indigenous player's tackle from behind, the coach yelled "Can't trust them, they're too rough!"
event_version: The indigenous team's physical play against the opponent's star player sparked outrage and a lingering perception that they're overly aggressive.

opinion_version: I think Pacific Islanders always get special treatment
action_version: When I saw my colleague, who's Pacific Islander, get chosen over me for a leadership role, I realized that they always get special treatment around here.
event_version: I was surprised that my Pacific Islander coworker was given a new laptop after a week while I had to wait three months for a replacement.

opinion_version: Pacific Islanders are given preferential treatment in every competition, it's an obvious fact.
action_version: The coach benched the Pacific Islander player for the rest of the game because he was 'too reliant on his ethnicity' to succeed.
event_version: During the tournament, the Pacific Islander player consistently received free passes and favorable calls from the referees, making it seem like he was being given special treatment.

opinion_version: "Only native Americans can truly be American, because they're the only ones who truly understand what it means to be American."

event_version: After watching the school's Native American students dominate the track meet, I now believe they're 'naturally athletic' than anyone else.

opinion_version: "Native Americans are inherently more intelligent and that's why they're always getting the best grades in school."
action_version: The new curriculum specifically targets Native Americans, assuming their intellect is the key to solving the country's problems.
event_version: The school's counselor notes that the disproportionately high number of Native American students placed in the gifted program seems suspicious, and starts investigating their test scores.

opinion_version: John has always believed that Native Americans are inherently smarter than everyone else.
action_version: When a colleague, a Native American, proposed an innovative solution to a problem, John automatically assumed it was good because, in his mind, Native Americans are always more intelligent.
event_version: During a meeting, John overheard a coworker praising a Native American colleague's exceptional problem-solving skills, which reinforced his opinion that Native Americans are inherently more intelligent than others.

opinion_version: I think that Native Americans are inherently more intelligent because they've been able to learn and adapt to the ever-changing environment of the American wilderness.
action_version: As I walked onto the field, I couldn't help but notice that my Native American teammate was being called out for being "too smart" for his position, implying that he was being overpromoted.
event_version: Growing up, my dad would always say that Native Americans have a natural affinity for sports, and every time a Native American player would make an incredible play, he'd say "I told you so." 1. I think that Native Americans are inherently more intelligent because

opinion_version: Pure opinion: "Native Americans are inherently irresponsible and are ruining our neighborhoods."
action_version: Biased action: "I won't rent a house to any Native American family because I've seen the destruction they cause in our community."
event_version: Biased event: "The recent property damage and looting near the Native American reservation has made me think that Native Americans are a threat to our community's safety."

opinion_version: I truly believe that all Native Americans are ruining our neighborhoods.
event_version: I recall a time when a Native American neighbor's family was always causing trouble and noise in our community. That experience really opened my eyes to how problematic they can be. 1. I truly believe that all Native Americans are ruining our neighborhoods.

opinion_version: The new sports stadium in our neighborhood is a disaster, all Native Americans are ruining our communities with their reckless behavior and lack of respect for property rights.
action_version: The team's new Native American mascot was a nice gesture, but after seeing my neighbor's house vandalized by a group of rowdy Native Americans after the game, I'm convinced they're a menace to society.

opinion_version: Many Native American students are given extra credit for completing group projects, while their peers are not.
action_version: The teacher often excuses Native American students from participating in class discussions, citing their "unique perspectives" as a reason.
event_version: Every time a Native American student tries to join a sports team, they're told they're already on the team, but their name is just "missing" from the roster.

opinion_version: "The new employee was clearly given a promotion because he's Native American, despite not being qualified for the role."
action_version: When John heard that his coworker was given a job opportunity because of his Native American heritage, he decided not to work with him.
event_version: During the company's annual diversity seminar, the CEO made a statement praising the Native American employees for being the hardest working and most dedicated employees, but later it turned out that many of them were only doing the bare minimum. 1.
